message.content:
Apply rootAVD script to root the AVD.
  
Steps:
1. Change to rootAVD directory: cd /home/cwtrocks/android-avd/rootAVD
   
2. Run rootAVD script on the AVD:
   - Command: ./rootAVD.sh sdk36_rootavd
   - Or with specific Magisk APK: ./rootAVD.sh sdk36_rootavd <magisk.apk>
   
3. Monitor the output for:
   - Ramdisk patching progress
   - Magisk installation
   - Success/failure messages
   
4. Check if AVD files were modified:
   - Look for backup files created
   - Check ramdisk.img modification time
   
5. If API 36 is not officially supported and fails, try:
   - Using latest Magisk Canary build
   - Manual patching steps from rootAVD documentation
   
Capture all output and error messages.

attachment.content:
- coredump: Analyze an Android coredump file. Print a Java class and its members, or drill into any field by address.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- superpowers:brainstorm: Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead
- superpowers:execute-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:brainstorming
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- superpowers:receiving-code-review
- superpowers:requesting-code-review
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- superpowers:systematic-debugging
- superpowers:test-driven-development
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees
- superpowers:using-superpowers
- superpowers:verification-before-completion
- superpowers:writing-plans
- superpowers:writing-skills
- claude-mem:babysit
- claude-mem:design-is
- claude-mem:do
- claude-mem:how-it-works
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent
- claude-mem:learn-codebase
- claude-mem:make-plan
- claude-mem:mem-search
- claude-mem:oh-my-issues
- claude-mem:pathfinder
- claude-mem:smart-explore
- claude-mem:timeline-report
- claude-mem:version-bump
- claude-mem:weekly-digests
- claude-mem:wowerpoint
- understand-anything:understand
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review
- security-review

input.description: Run rootAVD script on sdk36_rootavd AVD

content.content:
[1mrootAVD A Script to root AVD by NewBit XDA(B[m

Usage:	[1mrootAVD [DIR/ramdisk.img] [OPTIONS] | [EXTRA ARGUMENTS](B[m
or:	[1mrootAVD [ARGUMENTS](B[m

Arguments:
	[1mListAllAVDs(B[m			Lists Command Examples for ALL installed AVDs

	[1mInstallApps(B[m			Just install all APKs placed in the Apps folder

Main operation mode:
	[1mDIR(B[m				a path to an AVD system-image
					- must always be the [1m1st(B[m Argument after rootAVD
	
ADB Path | Ramdisk DIR | ANDROID_HOME:
	[1m[M]ac/Darwin:(B[m			export PATH=~/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools:$PATH
					export PATH=$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH
					system-images/android-$API/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/
	
	[1m[L]inux:(B[m			export PATH=~/Android/Sdk/platform-tools:$PATH
					export PATH=$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH
					system-images/android-$API/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/
	
	[1m[W]indows:(B[m			set PATH=%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk\platform-tools;%PATH%
					set PATH=%ANDROID_HOME%\platform-tools;%PATH%
					system-images\android-$API\google_apis_playstore\x86_64\
	
	[1mANDROID_HOME:(B[m			By default, the script uses [1m~/Android/Sdk(B[m, to set its Android Home
					directory, search for AVD system-images and ADB binarys. This behaviour
					can be overwritten by setting the ANDROID_HOME variable.
					e.g. [1mexport ANDROID_HOME=~/Downloads/sdk(B[m
	
	[1m$API:(B[m				25,29,30,31,32,33,34,UpsideDownCake,etc.
	
Options:
	[1mrestore(B[m				restore all existing [1m.backup(B[m files, but doesn't delete them
					- the AVD doesn't need to be running
					- no other Argument after will be processed
	
	[1mInstallKernelModules(B[m		install [1mcustom build kernel and its modules(B[m into ramdisk.img
					- kernel (bzImage) and its modules (initramfs.img) are inside rootAVD
					- both files will be deleted after installation
	
	[1mInstallPrebuiltKernelModules(B[m	download and install an [1mAOSP prebuilt kernel and its modules(B[m into ramdisk.img
					- similar to [1mInstallKernelModules(B[m, but the AVD needs to be online
	
	[1mAddRCscripts(B[m			install all custom *.rc scripts, placed in the rootAVD folder, into ramdisk.img/overlay.d/sbin
	
Options are [1mexclusive(B[m, only one at the time will be processed.
	
Extra Arguments:
	[1mDEBUG(B[m				[1mDebugging Mode(B[m, prevents rootAVD to pull back any patched file
	
	[1mPATCHFSTAB(B[m			[1mfstab.ranchu(B[m will get patched to automount Block Devices like [1m/dev/block/sda1(B[m
					- other entries can be added in the script as well
					- a custom build Kernel might be necessary
	
	[1mGetUSBHPmodZ(B[m			The [1mUSB HOST Permissions Module Zip(B[m will be downloaded into [1m/sdcard/Download(B[m
	
	[1mFAKEBOOTIMG(B[m			Creates a [1mfake Boot.img(B[m file that can directly be patched from the [1mMagisk APP(B[m
					- Magisk will be launched to patch the fake Boot.img [1mwithin 60s(B[m
					- the fake Boot.img will be placed under [1m/sdcard/Download/fakeboot.img(B[m
	
Extra Commands can be [1mcombined(B[m, there is no particular order.
	
[1mNotes: rootAVD will(B[m
- always create [1m.backup(B[m files of [1mramdisk.img(B[m and [1mkernel-ranchu(B[m
- [1mreplace(B[m both when done patching
- show a [1mMenu(B[m, to choose the Magisk Version [1m(Stable || Canary || Alpha)(B[m, if the AVD is [1monline(B[m
- make the [1mchoosen(B[m Magisk Version to its [1mlocal(B[m
- install all APKs placed in the Apps folder
- use [1m$ANDROID_HOME(B[m to search for AVD system images
	
[1mCommand Examples:(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh ListAllAVDs(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh InstallApps(B[m

[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img FAKEBOOTIMG(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img DEBUG PATCHFSTAB GetUSBHPmodZ(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img restore(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallKernelModules(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallPrebuiltKernelModules(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallPrebuiltKernelModules GetUSBHPmodZ PATCHFSTAB DEBUG(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36-ext19/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img AddRCscripts(B[m

content.text: The script is still showing the usage menu. Let me check if the AVD needs to be running first, and also try using the ListAllAVDs option to see what the script expects.

input.description: List all AVDs to see expected command format

content.content:
[1mrootAVD A Script to root AVD by NewBit XDA(B[m

Usage:	[1mrootAVD [DIR/ramdisk.img] [OPTIONS] | [EXTRA ARGUMENTS](B[m
or:	[1mrootAVD [ARGUMENTS](B[m

Arguments:
	[1mListAllAVDs(B[m			Lists Command Examples for ALL installed AVDs

	[1mInstallApps(B[m			Just install all APKs placed in the Apps folder

Main operation mode:
	[1mDIR(B[m				a path to an AVD system-image
					- must always be the [1m1st(B[m Argument after rootAVD
	
ADB Path | Ramdisk DIR | ANDROID_HOME:
	[1m[M]ac/Darwin:(B[m			export PATH=~/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools:$PATH
					export PATH=$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH
					system-images/android-$API/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/
	
	[1m[L]inux:(B[m			export PATH=~/Android/Sdk/platform-tools:$PATH
					export PATH=$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH
					system-images/android-$API/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/
	
	[1m[W]indows:(B[m			set PATH=%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk\platform-tools;%PATH%
					set PATH=%ANDROID_HOME%\platform-tools;%PATH%
					system-images\android-$API\google_apis_playstore\x86_64\
	
	[1mANDROID_HOME:(B[m			By default, the script uses [1m~/Android/Sdk(B[m, to set its Android Home
					directory, search for AVD system-images and ADB binarys. This behaviour
					can be overwritten by setting the ANDROID_HOME variable.
					e.g. [1mexport ANDROID_HOME=~/Downloads/sdk(B[m
	
	[1m$API:(B[m				25,29,30,31,32,33,34,UpsideDownCake,etc.
	
Options:
	[1mrestore(B[m				restore all existing [1m.backup(B[m files, but doesn't delete them
					- the AVD doesn't need to be running
					- no other Argument after will be processed
	
	[1mInstallKernelModules(B[m		install [1mcustom build kernel and its modules(B[m into ramdisk.img
					- kernel (bzImage) and its modules (initramfs.img) are inside rootAVD
					- both files will be deleted after installation
	
	[1mInstallPrebuiltKernelModules(B[m	download and install an [1mAOSP prebuilt kernel and its modules(B[m into ramdisk.img
					- similar to [1mInstallKernelModules(B[m, but the AVD needs to be online
	
	[1mAddRCscripts(B[m			install all custom *.rc scripts, placed in the rootAVD folder, into ramdisk.img/overlay.d/sbin
	
Options are [1mexclusive(B[m, only one at the time will be processed.
	
Extra Arguments:
	[1mDEBUG(B[m				[1mDebugging Mode(B[m, prevents rootAVD to pull back any patched file
	
	[1mPATCHFSTAB(B[m			[1mfstab.ranchu(B[m will get patched to automount Block Devices like [1m/dev/block/sda1(B[m
					- other entries can be added in the script as well
					- a custom build Kernel might be necessary
	
	[1mGetUSBHPmodZ(B[m			The [1mUSB HOST Permissions Module Zip(B[m will be downloaded into [1m/sdcard/Download(B[m
	
	[1mFAKEBOOTIMG(B[m			Creates a [1mfake Boot.img(B[m file that can directly be patched from the [1mMagisk APP(B[m
					- Magisk will be launched to patch the fake Boot.img [1mwithin 60s(B[m
					- the fake Boot.img will be placed under [1m/sdcard/Download/fakeboot.img(B[m
	
Extra Commands can be [1mcombined(B[m, there is no particular order.
	
[1mNotes: rootAVD will(B[m
- always create [1m.backup(B[m files of [1mramdisk.img(B[m and [1mkernel-ranchu(B[m
- [1mreplace(B[m both when done patching
- show a [1mMenu(B[m, to choose the Magisk Version [1m(Stable || Canary || Alpha)(B[m, if the AVD is [1monline(B[m
- make the [1mchoosen(B[m Magisk Version to its [1mlocal(B[m
- install all APKs placed in the Apps folder
- use [1m$ANDROID_HOME(B[m to search for AVD system images
	
[1mCommand Examples:(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh ListAllAVDs(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh InstallApps(B[m

[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img FAKEBOOTIMG(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img DEBUG PATCHFSTAB GetUSBHPmodZ(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img restore(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallKernelModules(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallPrebuiltKernelModules(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallPrebuiltKernelModules GetUSBHPmodZ PATCHFSTAB DEBUG(B[m
[1m./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-36/google_apis/x86_64/ramdisk.img AddRCscripts(B[m
